auth.totpHint: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
auth.resetSent: If the email exists, a reset link was sent.
auth.passwordUpdatedLogin: Password updated. You can now log in.
auth.noAccessToken: Login succeeded but no access token was returned.
auth.sessionLoadFailed: Logged in but could not load your session. Check the browser console or Railway logs.
auth.totpNoAccessToken: TOTP verified but no access token was returned.
auth.totpSessionLoadFailed: TOTP verified but could not load your session. The API may still be deploying — wait a moment and try again. Check the browser console for details.

settings.title: Profile
settings.lead: Your account details, password, authenticator, and language.
settings.totpEnrollHint: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code to confirm.
settings.languageLead: Choose the language used across the portal for your account.
settings.languageSavedLocally: Language updated on this device. Server settings are not available yet.

errors.network: Network error — could not reach the API. The backend may be redeploying or temporarily unavailable.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.greeting: Hi {{name}} — here’s your cloud spend overview.
dashboard.selectContext: Select a company and connection in the top bar to view daily total cost.
dashboard.topSpendSpikes: Top spend spikes vs recent baseline
dashboard.noSpendSpikes: No spend spikes in the last 30 days.

chart.noSeriesData: No series data for this range.

costExplorer.title: Cost Explorer
costExplorer.lead: Explore daily spend by service, scope, or top resources. Click a service/scope to filter the trend, or a top resource for cost + utilization.

recommendations.title: Recommendations
recommendations.lead: Where resources cost more than they’re used — with the fix and the savings.

anomalies.title: Cost Anomalies
anomalies.lead: Days where spend jumped well above its recent trend — what changed, by how much, and what drove it.

unitEconomics.title: Unit Economics
unitEconomics.lead: Divide your cloud bill by a business count you care about (customers, seats, orders) to see cost each.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.lead: Synced cloud resources for the selected connection — browse by type and compartment.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.lead: Utilization and performance metrics for resources in the selected connection.

reports.title: Reports
reports.lead: Download a CSV cost report for the selected date range.

connections.title: Connections
connections.lead: Cloud account connections for this company. OCI is available today; AWS and GCP will appear here when their connectors ship.

budgets.title: Budgets & Alerts
budgets.lead: Set spend caps and email preferences. Budgets and alerts are stored on the server.

allocations.title: Allocations
allocations.lead: Map spend to teams and products with tags and chargeback rules. Tag sync is coming next.

dashboard.intro: The dashboard summarizes cloud spend, savings opportunities, and unusual cost changes.
dashboard.howTo1: Select a company and cloud connection from the top bar.
dashboard.howTo2: Choose a cloud and date range to focus the cost view.
dashboard.see1: Total and average daily spend for the selected period.
dashboard.see2: The services and compartments driving the most cost.
dashboard.see3: Active recommendations that may reduce monthly spend.
dashboard.see4: Recent cost spikes compared with the usual baseline.
dashboard.footer: Open any section to investigate its details.

costExplorer.intro: Cost Explorer is for interactive cost analysis: totals, daily trend, and breakdowns by service, scope, and top resources — plus a resource drill-down that pairs spend with utilization.
costExplorer.howTo3: Click a service or scope bar to filter the daily trend to that slice.
costExplorer.howTo4: Open Top resources and click a resource for its daily cost. Compute instances also show CPU/memory vs provisioned capacity, with under (20%) / over (80%) guide lines.
costExplorer.scopeBody: Scope is the cloud account hierarchy slice: compartment in OCI, account in AWS, subscription / resource group in Azure. Filtering by cloud provider across connections is planned for a later pass.
costExplorer.whenBody: Start here when you need to answer “where did the money go?” or “is this expensive resource actually busy?” For CSV export, use Reports. For cost-per-customer style KPIs, use Unit Economics.

recommendations.intro: Recommendations highlight actionable savings and performance opportunities from monitoring, usage, and inventory signals.
recommendations.howTo1: Compare estimated monthly savings with performance alerts.
recommendations.howTo2: Filter by action, status, resource type, or scope.
recommendations.howTo3: Review the suggested action, resource signals, and current versus projected cost.
recommendations.howTo4: Silence an item for 30 days, ignore it until restored, or validate it in Inventory and Monitoring before acting.
recommendations.confidenceBody: Low confidence usually means limited or noisy metric history. Unattached-volume findings use attachment state and are typically stronger signals; treat utilization findings as leads to investigate.

anomalies.intro: Cost Anomalies highlight unusual spend changes against the recent baseline, including spikes that may indicate new workloads, runaway usage, or misconfiguration.
anomalies.howTo1: Choose the date range to scan.
anomalies.howTo2: Investigate anomalies with the largest changes first.
anomalies.howTo3: Use the driver service as a starting point in Cost Explorer.
anomalies.footer: Use Budgets & Alerts to receive email when similar events occur.

unitEconomics.intro: Unit Economics divides your cloud bill by a business count to show cost per customer, seat, order, or another unit.
unitEconomics.example: For example, a $12,000 bill divided by 120 active customers equals $100 per customer.
unitEconomics.formula: cost each = total cloud bill for the date range ÷ business count
unitEconomics.howTo1: Add a business count, its singular label, and its current value.
unitEconomics.howTo2: Choose the date range that determines the cloud bill.
unitEconomics.howTo3: Review Cloud cost each for the bill divided by that count.
unitEconomics.terms1: How many is a value from your business, such as customers, seats, or API calls.
unitEconomics.terms2: Counted as is the singular label displayed with the result.
unitEconomics.limits1: The calculation uses the full bill for the selected connection.
unitEconomics.limits2: It does not yet split cost by cloud, service, resource, or tag.
unitEconomics.limits3: Product-level cost per unit will require Allocations and tags.
unitEconomics.footer: Use it for infrastructure cost per customer, seat, order, or similar business measure.

reports.intro: Reports exports a CSV cost report for the selected connection and date range for finance, audits, or offline analysis.
reports.footer: Use Cost Explorer for interactive analysis and Budgets & Alerts for recurring governance.

inventory.intro: Inventory is the catalog of synced compute, storage, networking, and related cloud resources for the selected connection.
inventory.howTo1: Switch tabs to browse a resource family.
inventory.howTo2: Filter resources by compartment when available.
inventory.howTo3: Confirm what exists before acting on a recommendation.
inventory.footer: Monitoring shows runtime metrics, while Recommendations uses those signals to identify opportunities.

monitoring.intro: Monitoring shows utilization and performance metrics for resources in the selected connection.
monitoring.howTo1: Choose a resource type, resource, metric, and date range.
monitoring.howTo2: Load metrics to view the chart and mean, minimum, and maximum values.
monitoring.howTo3: Select a resource name in the table to open its chart.
monitoring.howTo4: Compute charts include provisioned capacity and 20% and 80% utilization guides.
monitoring.footer: Capacity gauges may stay flat; use CPU and throughput to understand activity, or Cost Explorer to compare cost and utilization.

connections.intro: Connections let Koltto authenticate to cloud accounts and scope cost, inventory, and monitoring sync.
connections.howTo1: Create a connection with the credentials required by the cloud provider.
connections.howTo2: Select the connection in the top bar when working with Costs or Resources.
connections.howTo3: A company can have multiple connections across cloud providers.
connections.footer: Cost features currently use the active connection’s synced data.

budgets.intro: Budgets & Alerts sets spend caps and email notifications so cost surprises can be detected automatically.
budgets.howTo1: Create a budget with an amount, period, and account, service, or compartment scope.
budgets.howTo2: Set the percentage at which an alert should fire.
budgets.howTo3: Use a template to start from a common pattern.
budgets.alertsBody: Choose which anomalies, recommendations, and budget threshold events send email and who receives them.
budgets.nextBody: Tag and organization scopes will arrive with Allocations; current scopes apply to the selected connection.

allocations.intro: Allocations will map cloud spend to teams, products, and cost centers using tags and allocation rules.
allocations.howTo3: Unit economics filtered to a product or environment.
allocations.howTo4: Comparable costs across clouds using shared tags.
allocations.statusBody: This page is a placeholder until tag sync and allocation rules are available.
allocations.footer: Tags attribute spend; business metrics turn attributed spend into cost per unit.

advice.idle: Stop or terminate this instance — you're paying for compute that isn't being used.
advice.oversized: Downsize to a smaller shape (roughly half the OCPUs/memory).
advice.overutilized: Upsize to a larger shape (or scale out) to protect performance.
advice.idleLb: Terminate this load balancer if it's no longer serving traffic.
advice.idleVolume: Review it — if nothing needs this volume, terminate it.
advice.idleBootVolume: Review it — if nothing needs this boot volume, terminate it.
advice.idleFileSystem: Review it — if nothing needs this file system, terminate it.
advice.unattachedBlock: Terminate this block volume if nothing needs the data, or attach it to an instance if it is still required.
advice.unattachedBoot: Terminate this boot volume if nothing needs the data, or attach it to an instance if it is still required.

recommendations.selectContext: Select a company and cloud connection in the top bar to see recommendations.
recommendations.empty: No recommendations for these filters. Try another status, or sync cost and monitoring for the last 30 days.

costExplorer.selectContext: Select a company and cloud connection in the top bar.
costExplorer.byServiceHint: Click a service to filter the daily trend.
costExplorer.byScopeHint: Click a scope to filter the daily trend. On OCI this is a compartment; on AWS an account; on Azure a subscription or resource group.
costExplorer.topResourcesHint: Click a resource to open cost + utilization (compute shows capacity and 20%/80% guides).

anomalies.selectContext: Select a company and cloud connection in the top bar to see anomalies.
anomalies.empty: No spend spikes in the last 30 days — daily cost stayed within its normal range.

inventory.selectContext: Select a company and connection from the top bar.
inventory.empty: No resources found for this selection.

monitoring.selectContext: Select a company and connection from the top bar.
monitoring.info: Sync monitoring from Inventory, then choose a resource type and resource to chart.
monitoring.empty: Pick a resource type and resource, then load metrics to see the chart.
monitoring.selectResource: Select a resource to see its time-series chart.

reports.selectContext: Select a company and cloud connection in the top bar.
reports.empty: No report data is available for this date range.

budgets.selectContext: Select a company and cloud connection in the top bar.
budgets.empty: No budgets yet. Create one or use a template.
budgets.name: Name

unitEconomics.selectContext: Select a company and cloud connection in the top bar.
unitEconomics.fullBillHint: Uses the full bill for the selected connection and date range.
unitEconomics.empty: No counts yet. Add a business count below.
unitEconomics.businessCountsHint: These values come from your business, not from the cloud.

connections.selectContext: Select a company from the top bar.

allocations.empty: Allocations are coming soon. Tag sync and allocation rules will map cloud spend to teams, products, and cost centers.

modals.cancelAllSyncsConfirm: Cancel all open inventory runs and abort all queued/in-progress sync jobs across every company?
modals.resyncMonitoringConfirm: Enqueue monitoring re-sync for all companies (every compartment × resource type, last ~90 days)? Jobs appear under Open syncs. Syncs must not be paused.
